articles.title: PVR Inox Q4 Results: Firm posts net profit of ₹186 crore against loss YoY; revenue jumps 26%
articles.summary: PVR Inox Q4 Results: Net profit at ₹186.7 crore as against net loss of ₹125 crore YoY

articles.title: NSE SME stock hits upper circuit despite stock market crash
articles.summary: Atmastco share price hit a 5% upper circuit on 11 May after receiving BIS certification for Level 6 Bulletproof Jackets, marking a significant growth milestone. The company aims to enhance its presence in the defence sector and to start commercial production soon.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: UPL Q4 Results: Profit jumps 18% YoY to ₹1,061 crore; company announces 300% dividend payout
articles.summary: UPL Limited reported an 18% YoY increase in consolidated net profit to ₹1,061 crore for Q4 FY 2025-26, up from ₹896 crore last year. The QoQ profit rose 168% from ₹396 crore in the previous quarter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Canara Bank Q4 results 2026: Profit declines 10% YoY to ₹4506 crore; recommends dividend of ₹4.20
articles.summary: Canara Bank Q4 results 2026: Canara Bank reported a 10% YoY fall in standalone profit to ₹4,505.57 crore. NII rose by nearly 4% YoY and 6% QoQ to ₹9,808 crore, while NIM eased to 2.54% in Q4FY26 from 2.73% YoY.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Explained: Why PM Modi asked Indians to avoid buying gold for a year and what it means for prices
articles.summary: It's no secret that Indians have a long affinity for gold. We are also one of the world's largest consumers, typically importing 90% of the domestic demand. These massive gold imports form a major part of our import bill, especially now that rising prices are also increasing import costs.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Investors return to FMCG stocks as early signs of demand recovery emerge
articles.summary: Early signs of demand recovery and improving earnings outlook drive renewed interest in FMCG stocks after a prolonged phase of underperformance.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market crash: Nifty 50 slips below 24,000. Will it fall below 22,000 this time?
articles.summary: Nifty 50 has declined 0.5% over the past one month and has fallen nearly 7.7% in the last three months. Monday’s decline below the crucial 24,000 mark has further weakened near-term sentiment, with broader market breadth also turning negative.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: SBI: The worst of margin pressure could be over after Q4’s sharp sequential drop
articles.summary: SBI shares slid 10% in two sessions after Q4 results as weak NII growth and margin pressure overshadowed strong core operating profit and steady asset quality. Investors weigh treasury trade-offs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Life insurance stocks gain after strong FY27 start; SBI Life, ICICI Prudential lead growth
articles.summary: Life insurance stocks showed slight gains on May 11, with SBI Life and ICICI Prudential leading the way. The sector experienced robust growth in FY27, particularly SBI Life with an 80% increase in new business premiums and LIC with a 38% rise.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vodafone Idea, YES Bank, Ola Electric, HFCL among the most traded stocks on NSE today, 11 May 2026
articles.summary: Most traded stocks today: Meanwhile, Vodafone Idea, YES Bank, HFCL, Ola Electric Mobility, GTL Infrastructure, Rain Industries, and PC Jeweller  were among the most traded stocks, or most active stocks in terms of volume, on the NSE.

articles.summary: Canara Bank Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: The bank’s net interest income (NII), the difference between interest earned and interest paid, in Q4FY26 increased 4% to ₹9,808 crore from ₹9,442 crore, YoY.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests NLC India, Apollo Hospitals shares to buy
articles.summary: India's benchmark indices fell on May 11, with Nifty 50 down 1.04%. The rupee weakened amid rising oil prices. While large caps struggled, mid and small-cap stocks showed strong performance, signaling a potential shift in market dynamics.

articles.title: PVR Inox Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: Firm posts profit of ₹186 crore vs loss YoY; average ticket price jumps 22%
articles.summary: PVR Inox Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: PVR Inox shares crashed 4% after Q4 results announcement. The company posted a profit as opposed to a loss in the year-ago period.

articles.title: Copper Shrugs Off Middle East Uncertainty to Aim for Record High
articles.summary: Copper headed for its highest-ever close and other metals advanced as traders shrugged off the apparent deadlock between the US and Iran to join a broader rally for risk assets.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil Market in ‘Race Against Time’ on Hormuz, Morgan Stanley Says
articles.summary: The oil market is in “a race against time” as the factors that combined to restrain price rises from the Iran war so far may no longer hold if the Strait of Hormuz stays closed into June, according to Morgan Stanley.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Nifty Bank crashes nearly 1,000 points, IT pack resilient amid stock market fall: What's behind this trend?
articles.summary: The flagship Nifty Bank index tumbled 1.70% in intraday deals today, as barring Bank of Baroda (BoB), all stocks faced intense selling pressure. State Bank of India (SBI) emerged as the biggest laggard as it extended its post-earnings decline.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Value 360 Communications share price hits 5% upper circuit after weak debut
articles.summary: Value 360 Communications debuted on NSE SME at ₹78.40, 20% below the issue price. The stock reached a high of ₹82.30 after hitting an intraday low of ₹74.50. The IPO was oversubscribed 1.20 times, driven mainly by institutional investors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: JBM Auto, Ola Electric Mobility, and Ather Energy: Why these auto stocks rising after PM Modi’s speech?
articles.summary: JBM Auto share price jumped as much as 7.26%, Ola Electric shares surged 4.49%, Ather Energy stock price spiked 5.6%, while Olectra Greentech shares rallied 4.9%.

articles.title: SpiceJet to IndiGo: Why are aviation stocks down up to 5% today? Explained
articles.summary: Aviation stocks like SpiceJet, Interglobe Aviation (IndiGo), GMR Airports share price fell up to 5% in Monday's trading session as crude oil prices and PM Modi's appeal to avoid international travel weighed on sentiments.

articles.title: Vodafone Idea share price zooms over 10% despite stock market crash
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea share price surged as much as 9.96% to ₹12.37 apiece in Monday's trading session. Here's what's behind the rally, along with share price trend.

articles.summary: Gold price today remained steady across major Indian cities as 24K, 22K gold and silver rates showed limited movement amid rising geopolitical tensions and strong safe-haven demand.

articles.summary: Ambani's ​Jio Platforms, which houses the world's second-largest telecom company by users after China Mobile, counts Meta, Google and Vista Equity Partners among its investors.

articles.title: Chalet Hotels, Lemon Tree to IHCL: Hotel stocks drop up to 3% on PM Modi's appeal
articles.summary: Shares of major hospitality companies fell over 2% on May 11 following Prime Minister Modi's call for a temporary pause on foreign travel. He urged citizens to focus on domestic tourism to support the local economy during the ongoing global crisis.

articles.title: Sensex crashes almost 1,200 points; investors lose ₹4 lakh crore: Key factors behind stock market crash explained

articles.title: Rupee opens 40 paise lower at 94.88 against the US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to 94.88 against the dollar as crude oil prices surged and U.S.-Iran tensions escalated. Experts note India's growing dollar dependence, despite healthy foreign reserves, affecting currency performance.

articles.title: Gold price falls on MCX as higher crude oil prices, stalled US–Iran peace talks fuel inflation worries
articles.summary: Higher crude oil prices and derailed US-Iran talks have fuelled inflation worries, strengthening the expectations of no rate cuts by the US Federal Reserve this year. Higher-for-longer interest rates are negative for non-yielding bullion.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.summary: The Indian stock market saw losses on 8 May, with Sensex down 516 points and Nifty 50 down 151 points due to banking stock weakness. Analysts suggest buying Metropolis Healthcare, Bharat Forge, and Timken India based on recovery trends and market analysis.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: PM's appeal: Diesel consumption in agriculture can be scaled back with use of alternative energy, experts say
articles.summary: Prime Minister Modi's appeal for reduced fuel use highlights agriculture's significant diesel consumption. Experts advocate for alternative energy sources like biofuels and compressed biogas to lower costs and improve farm profitability.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India inflation likely rose to 3.8% in April as food prices hardened: Mint poll
articles.summary: Economists expect CPI inflation to move closer to the RBI’s 4% target amid rising edible oil, vegetable and energy costs, though no immediate rate action is seen.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt capex drives private investment surge; CII urges price restraint
articles.summary: CII said the Centre’s capital expenditure push has revived private investment and order book growth across sectors, while urging phased rollback of fuel duty cuts and stronger support for MSMEs amid global uncertainty.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Fertilizer squeeze: Why soaring import costs are a policy tightrope for India
articles.summary: With urea prices up 81% and the kharif season looming, New Delhi must balance a ballooning subsidy bill against the urgent need to secure 50% of its remaining fertilizer requirements from a volatile global market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: India-Vietnam trade ties, missed direct tax target, PMI rebound
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Oil marketing companies lose ₹30,000 crore a month amid high crude prices: Govt
articles.summary: Pump price of petrol is currently at ₹94.77 per litre in Delhi, while diesel is being retailed at at ₹87.67 per litre. The domestic LPG cylinder is priced at ₹913. The government last revised retail prices of petrol and diesel in 2024, cutting them by ₹2 a litre ahead of the national elections.&nbsp
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: E-way bill generation eases in April after March peak
articles.summary: E-way bill generation still saw a 12% jump from April last year, showing sustained growth in economic activity and compliance
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil prices rise after US, Iran exchange fire at Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Oil prices rose after US and Iranian forces exchanged fire at the Strait of Hormuz, the first clash since the 7 April ceasefire, stoking fresh supply fears in a key global shipping lane.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: After BJP victory, Centre begins review of pending schemes in Bengal
articles.summary: A fractious relationship between the state and Centre—run by the Trinamool Congress TMC and the BJP-led National Democratic Alliance, respectively—meant several central schemes became mired in disputes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Parliamentary panel to review domestic output of petroleum products on 15 May
articles.summary: India’s parliamentary panel will review fuel and fertilizer supplies on 15 May amid disruptions caused by the West Asia war. While petrol and diesel stocks remain stable, LPG shortages persist due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, prompting booking restrictions and supply monitoring.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Broad-based sector stress drove ECLGS 5.0 rollout, says finance secretary
articles.summary: On Wednesday, the government approved the scheme with a total outlay of ₹2.55 trillion, including a dedicated ₹5,000 crore window for airlines. The scheme allows additional credit of up to 20% of peak working capital utilised for eligible borrowers affected by the conflict.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oil prices steady as US, Iran consider fresh proposal for peace deal
articles.summary: Oil prices steadied after an 8% drop as hopes of a US-Iran peace deal lifted sentiment, though conflicting signals from Donald Trump capped gains.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sebi’s policy u-turns raise questions on consultations, regulatory certainty
articles.summary: Sebi's recent policy changes have sparked concerns over regulatory uncertainty, with four decisions reversed after stakeholder feedback. Critics emphasize the importance of consistent consultation, which was missing in some two instances.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Welfare spending surge pushes subsidy outgo to 91% of target
articles.summary: Food subsidy, the largest component, stood at ₹1.92 trillion, or 85% of the revised estimate of ₹2.28 trillion. However, the urea subsidy has already breached its annual target, reaching ₹1.28 trillion, or 101% of the revised estimates, and higher than the ₹1.16 trillion recorded a year ago.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How India’s growth story has left its workers behind
articles.summary: Noida’s unrest highlights India’s structural crisis: skipping manufacturing for services has left a low-skilled workforce with stagnant real wages, poor living and working conditions, and diminishing bargaining power.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India’s services sector recovers lost ground, domestic market fuels growth in April: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: India's services sector showed recovery in April, with the HSBC PMI rising to 58.8. Increased output and new business orders were noted, alongside a shift to domestic suppliers. The composite PMI also rose to 58.2, indicating growth momentum across sectors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US Envoy Sergio Gor confirms Marco Rubio will visit India soon to ‘build even stronger ties’
articles.summary: US envoy Sergio Gor announced that Indian companies plan to invest over $20.5 billion in various sectors, contributing to job creation and stronger supply chains. The SelectUSA Investment Summit promotes foreign direct investment, connecting global business leaders with US economic developers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: West Asia war may briefly dent India's growth as fundamentals strong: Memani
articles.summary: CII President Rajiv Memani, who is also the regional managing partner of EY Africa-India and chair of EY Growth Markets Council, said that the challenge before businesses is to strike a balance between the costs that they can absorb and those that must be passed on to consumers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt clears ₹50,000-crore package to boost credit flow and support businesses
articles.summary: The government-backed loan support scheme will facilitate an additional credit flow of up to ₹2.55 trillion to the economy, including ₹5,000 crore specifically for the aviation sector, a government statement said.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: SCBs clock 15.9% credit growth in FY26, led by services, MSMEs, retail
articles.summary: The 497-basis-point jump reflects sustained economic momentum and rising demand for credit across sectors.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India plans to award construction-ready highway contracts, with all approvals in place, to curb delays
articles.summary: In a bid to eliminate persistent highway project delays, MoRTH is proposing stricter pre-award filters, ensuring all critical land and structural clearances are in place before contracts are awarded. This shift aims to make highway projects more execution-ready.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why RBI wants to keep India's gold at home
articles.summary: RBI repatriated over 100 tonnes of gold to India in the six months to March, taking the total gold reserves stored locally to 680 tonnes. Why is the central bank bringing gold back home?
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India among most resilient as global shocks test emerging markets: Moody’s Ratings
articles.summary: India and Thailand are well placed to manage future shocks because monetary policy frameworks are clear and predictable, inflation expectations are well anchored, and exchange rates can adjust when needed, Moody’s Ratings said.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India’s fertilizer subsidy reaches ₹2.17 trillion in FY26 due to West Asia war, may rise by a fifth in FY27
articles.summary: As the war in West Asia disrupts supply chains, India's fertilizer subsidy may exceed ₹2.17 trillion in FY27. This trend raises concerns about fiscal discipline and food inflation.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI reshuffles top deck: New DG Rohit Jain takes charge, portfolios realigned
articles.summary: Rohit Jain has been appointed deputy governor of the Reserve Bank of India, leading to a portfolio reshuffle among governors. The changes, effective 4 May, aim to enhance oversight of fintech and adapt to evolving financial priorities amid global volatility.
articles.label: BULLISH
